title: Identifying AWS Services
description:
## Which Service Provides Compute?

You need to launch a **resizable virtual server** on AWS — a machine where you pick the CPU and memory, install your own OS and software, and can resize it later. Which AWS service is the core, general-purpose **compute** offering for this?
explanation: **EC2 (Elastic Compute Cloud)** is AWS's core resizable virtual-machine service — you choose an instance type (CPU/memory), pick an OS image, and manage the server yourself. **S3** is object storage for files and blobs, not compute. **RDS** is a managed relational database service (MySQL, PostgreSQL, etc.). **Lambda** runs short-lived event-driven functions (serverless/FaaS), not long-lived resizable VMs. When you want a general-purpose virtual server on AWS, EC2 is the answer.

hints.title: Match the resource type
hints.body: Compute = virtual machines, storage = files/objects, database = managed DB engines, functions = serverless code. The question asks for a resizable virtual server.
